statements.__comment: Project `_ont_public` on GCPBucket. A bucket is considered unconditionally public when either (a) a legacy ACL or default object ACL exposes it to `allUsers` / `allAuthenticatedUsers` (captured at sync time as `acl_public`) or (b) it has at least one unconditional IAM policy binding tagged `is_public = true`. Buckets with `iam_config_public_access_prevention = 'enforced'` are never public regardless of binding state. Conditional bindings (`has_condition = true`) are excluded so we match the GCP bucket-public rule. Absence of both signals yields `false` so rules can rely on a non-null boolean.
statements.query: MATCH (b:GCPBucket) SET b._ont_public = CASE WHEN COALESCE(b.iam_config_public_access_prevention, '') = 'enforced' THEN false ELSE COALESCE(b.acl_public, false) OR EXISTS { MATCH (b)<-[:APPLIES_TO]-(binding:GCPPolicyBinding) WHERE binding.is_public = true AND COALESCE(binding.has_condition, false) = false } END
